replication_study.hypothesis: Among survey respondents, higher motivation for online distance learning (measured by online_learning_1; higher values indicate greater motivation) is associated with lower odds of being in the Severe K10 psychological distress category versus lower distress categories, controlling where possible for gender.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Post-cleaning subset after removing pre-launch test sessions per created timestamp filter in Analysis_updated.Rmd
data_plan.sample_size: 766 total rows in CSV; analytic N to be computed after K10 scoring and listwise deletion; expected N ~ 730–750 complete cases for K10 + online_learning_1.
data_plan.access_details: Provided in replication_data folder; analysis reads /app/data/AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the same core constructs and items as the original study (K10 items and an item on motivation for distance learning) collected independently in a different sample/time. It allows testing whether the inverse association between learning motivation and severe distress replicates in a new population.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both measure psychological distress using the 10-item Kessler scale with the same scoring and severity categorization rules (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf summary) and include an item on motivation for distance learning (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json; replication_data/Analysis_updated.Rmd imports online_learning_1 and constructs Kessler_categories).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data were Jordanian university students surveyed in May 2020 with N=381 and analyses run in SPSS (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf summary). The replication data were collected via Prolific in 2021 (Analysis_updated.Rmd references Prolific and filters created >= 2021-01-19), have larger N (766 rows), likely broader geography, and use R/mlogit analysis. The motivation scale is coded 1–4 (replication CSV), whereas the original reported a dichotomous contrast (higher vs lower motivation) for the focal OR (post_registration.json).

data_plan.notes: Variables: Kessler_1–Kessler_10 are 1–5 Likert; Kessler_3 and Kessler_6 follow skip-logic and are imputed to 1 if preceding item equals 1 (Analysis_updated.Rmd). online_learning_1 ranges 1–4. demographic_1 appears to be year of birth; demographic_2 likely gender (1/2). Timestamps are strings with timezone handling in R. Ensure consistent factor levels for Kessler_categories and optionally harmonize motivation to binary (e.g., high vs low) to match original OR if a direct comparison is desired.

steps: Apply skip-logic imputations: if Kessler_2==1 & Kessler_3 is NA then set Kessler_3=1; if Kessler_5==1 & Kessler_6 is NA then set Kessler_6=1.
Create K10 severity categories: No Distress (<20), Low (20–24), Moderate (25–29), Severe (>29). Set factor with ref level No Distress (as in Analysis_updated.Rmd).
Inspect distributions and confirm adequate cell counts across motivation levels.
Primary analysis A (close to original): Multinomial logit of K10 category on online_learning_1 (treated ordinal), ref=No Distress, using mlogit. Extract ORs and CIs for Severe vs No Distress.

Robustness B (closer to reported OR contrast): Recode motivation to binary (High vs Low; e.g., 3–4 vs 1–2) and re-estimate multinomial logit to obtain OR for Severe vs No Distress comparable to the original (OR~0.45).
Optional control: add gender (demographic_2) as covariate to check stability of the focal association.
Handle missing data via listwise deletion after scoring.
Document and save model summaries and ORs/CIs.
planned_method.models: Multinomial logistic regression (mlogit) with K10 severity categories as outcome; extract odds ratios for category contrasts (Severe vs No Distress primary).
planned_method.outcome_variable: K10 psychological distress severity category (No, Low, Moderate, Severe)
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: online_learning_1 (motivation for distance learning; 1–4). For robustness, a binary High vs Low motivation split.
planned_method.control_variables: Gender (demographic_2) if coding is verified; optionally age computed from year of birth (demographic_1) as sensitivity only.
planned_method.tools_software: R (RMarkdown). Packages: rio, mlogit, knitr/rmarkdown; openssl only for de-identification (not needed for analysis).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Odds ratios for Severe vs No Distress per one-unit increase in motivation (and for High vs Low motivation in robustness).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z tests from multinomial logit coefficients; 95% confidence intervals via confint.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Apply skip-logic imputations for K3/K6, then listwise deletion on outcome and predictors used in each model.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Focus on the pre-registered focal contrast (Severe vs No Distress). Secondary contrasts reported without multiplicity correction; interpret cautiously.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided alpha=0.05. Replication success emphasized on direction (negative association), statistical significance (p<0.05), and OR magnitude reasonably near the original (e.g., within original 95% CI or a predefined tolerance).

files.Analysis.Rmd: Early draft illustrating mlogit usage and a placeholder import of fulldata.csv; not used for the final replication.
files.Analysis_updated.Rmd: Main RMarkdown pipeline: loads data, applies K10 skip-logic imputations, computes K10 total and categories, and runs multinomial logistic regression of Kessler_categories on online_learning_1 with ref=No Distress. File has been updated to read data from /app/data.
files.AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv: De-identified replication dataset with Kessler items, online_learning_1, and demographics. Primary analysis input.
files.AlTammemi_Survey_example.csv: Small example dataset mirroring column structure; for illustration/testing only.

codebase.notes: Ensure the working directory allows RMarkdown to import from /app/data. Analysis_updated.Rmd has been modified to use absolute path /app/data/AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv. No files are written by the analysis by default; if saving outputs, write them to /app/data.

analysis.instructions: From an R environment with the listed packages, render data/original/22/input/replication_data/Analysis_updated.Rmd or source the code chunks interactively. Ensure /app/data/AlTammemi_Survey_deidentify.csv is present. Extract model summary, exponentiate coefficients (exp()) for ORs, and use confint() for CIs. For the robustness binary split on motivation, create a recoded variable (e.g., High=3–4; Low=1–2) and re-run the model.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction of association (OR<1 expected), statistical significance (p<0.05), and effect size proximity to the original OR=0.45 (95% CI: 0.28–0.70; initial_details.txt; post_registration.json). Also compare whether replication CI overlaps the original CI and whether conclusions match (inverse relationship).
